prerequisites: A reachable Oracle Database instance (Airbyte documents support for 11g and above, including 19c, 21c, and 23ai) with network connectivity from the machine running the connector.
The database host and port (Oracle listens on port 1521 by default).
A connection identifier: either a Service Name or a System ID (SID), supplied through the connection_data config field.
A database user (username) and password. A dedicated read-only user is recommended.

The user must at minimum be able to log in and read the target tables: GRANT CREATE SESSION TO <user>; plus GRANT SELECT ANY TABLE TO <user>; (or per-table GRANT SELECT ON "<schema>"."<table>" TO <user>;).
The schemas to replicate (schemas config field). Values are case-sensitive; if left empty it defaults to the upper-cased username.
If connecting through a bastion via SSH tunnel: the jump-server host, SSH port (default 22), an SSH login (OS) username, and either an SSH private key or the SSH user's password.
If using TLS encryption: the Oracle server's SSL certificate in PEM format.

authMethods.name: Database username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to Oracle with a database user (username config field) and its password (password secret field). Airbyte recommends a dedicated read-only user granted CREATE SESSION and SELECT on the tables to sync.

authMethods.name: TLS encrypted connection with server certificate
authMethods.summary: Set the encryption config field to TLS Encrypted and provide the Oracle server's SSL certificate in PEM format via the encryption.ssl_certificate secret field. The connector also supports Native Network Encryption (NNE) with an algorithm such as AES256, and an Unencrypted mode (self-managed only).

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel key authentication
authMethods.summary: Reach Oracle through a bastion using SSH Key Authentication. Provide the jump-server host, SSH port, and SSH login username under tunnel_method, and supply the RSA private key (PEM) via the tunnel_method.ssh_key secret field. This authenticates the tunnel, not the database; database username/password are still required.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel password authentication
authMethods.summary: Reach Oracle through a bastion using SSH Password Authentication. Provide the jump-server host, SSH port, and SSH login username under tunnel_method, and supply the bastion user's password via the tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password secret field. This authenticates the tunnel, not the database.

setupSteps.title: Create a dedicated read-only database user
setupSteps.body: Connect to Oracle and create a user for the connector, then grant login and read access. For example: CREATE USER airbyte IDENTIFIED BY <password>; GRANT CREATE SESSION TO airbyte; GRANT SELECT ANY TABLE TO airbyte; For tighter scope, grant SELECT on specific tables instead: GRANT SELECT ON "<schema>"."<table>" TO airbyte;

setupSteps.title: Set host, port, and connection identifier
setupSteps.body: Enter the database host and port (default 1521) in the host and port config fields. In connection_data choose how to identify the database: a Service Name (recommended for modern Oracle, 8i and later) or a System ID (SID) for legacy single-instance databases.

setupSteps.title: Provide database credentials
setupSteps.body: Set the username config field to the database user and supply its password in the password secret field. Optionally set schemas to a case-sensitive list of schemas to replicate; if omitted it defaults to the upper-cased username.

setupSteps.title: Choose an encryption mode
setupSteps.body: Set the encryption config field to one of: Unencrypted (plain TCP, self-managed deployments only), Native Network Encryption (NNE) with an algorithm such as AES256, or TLS Encrypted. For TLS, paste the Oracle server certificate in PEM format into the encryption.ssl_certificate secret field.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel if required
setupSteps.body: If Oracle is only reachable through a bastion, set tunnel_method to SSH Key Authentication or Password Authentication and provide the jump-server host, SSH port (default 22), and the SSH login (OS) username. For key auth supply the RSA private key (PEM) in tunnel_method.ssh_key; for password auth supply the bastion user's password in tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password. Generate a key pair with: ssh-keygen -t rsa -m PEM -f myuser_rsa

setupSteps.title: Set optional JDBC parameters and verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Optionally add extra JDBC driver options in jdbc_url_params as key=value pairs joined by & (for example oracle.net.CONNECT_TIMEOUT=10000&defaultRowPrefetch=20). Leave check_privileges enabled to have the connector validate table-read permissions during setup, then test the connection.

sources.title: Airbyte Docs: Oracle DB Source Connector Setup Guide
